skill.name: slack-bot-builder
skill.description: Build Slack apps using the Bolt framework across Python, JavaScript, and Java. Covers Block Kit for rich UIs, interactive components, slash commands, event handling, OAuth installation flows, and workspace deployment.
skill.summary: Build Slack apps using the Bolt framework across Python, JavaScript, and Java with secure authentication and interactive UIs.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation containing educational code examples for the Slack Bolt framework. Environment variable usage follows security best practices for credential handling. No executable code with security risks was detected.

evidence.reasoning: Python Bolt framework initialization code, not shell command execution
Continuation of Bolt app setup examples
Block Kit UI building examples using Python dictionaries
Documentation URL reference to Slack Block Kit Builder tool
Slack API client.views_open() method call, not file system operations
Reading SLACK_BOT_TOKEN from environment - recommended secure practice
Reading SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET from environment - recommended secure practice
Reading SLACK_BOT_TOKEN from environment in Block Kit example
Reading SLACK_CLIENT_ID from environment - standard OAuth configuration
Reading SLACK_CLIENT_SECRET from environment - standard OAuth configuration

content.user_title: Build Slack Apps with Bolt Framework
content.value_statement: Building Slack integrations requires handling authentication, events, and complex UI patterns. This skill provides ready-to-use Bolt framework templates for Python, JavaScript, and Java that handle security, OAuth flows, and interactive Block Kit components.
actual_capabilities: Create Bolt apps in Python, JavaScript, and Java with proper initialization
Build interactive Block Kit UIs with buttons, menus, and modals
Implement slash commands with proper acknowledgment patterns
Handle Slack events and message patterns with custom logic
Configure OAuth 2.0 flows for multi-workspace distribution
Design incident notification templates with action buttons
limitations: Requires Slack app creation and token configuration in Slack API dashboard
Bolt framework dependencies must be installed separately for each language
Cannot bypass Slack API rate limits or platform restrictions
Production deployments require hosting infrastructure for HTTP endpoints

use_cases.title: DevOps Incident Alerting
use_cases.description: Create Slack bots that post formatted incident alerts with severity indicators and action buttons for acknowledging, escalating, or resolving incidents directly from Slack.

use_cases.title: Team Notification Bot
use_cases.description: Build bots that send automated notifications for CI/CD pipelines, code reviews, or deployment status with interactive elements for quick team responses.

use_cases.title: Support Ticket System
use_cases.description: Implement slash commands and modals for creating, tracking, and updating support tickets with custom fields and assignment workflows inside Slack.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Message Handler
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Bolt app in Python that responds to messages containing specific keywords. Set up the app with environment variables for tokens, add a message listener for 'hello', and include a slash command that acknowledges the request and sends a follow-up message.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Bolt framework basics

prompt_templates.title: Interactive Button Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Block Kit message with action buttons for an incident notification. Include a header with severity indicator, fields for incident details, and buttons for Acknowledge, Escalate, and Resolve actions. Handle button click events with appropriate callbacks.

prompt_templates.title: Modal Form Collection
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a slash command that opens a modal with input blocks for ticket title, description, and priority selection. Handle the view submission to extract form data and create a ticket confirmation message with the submitted values.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building forms for data collection in Slack

prompt_templates.title: OAuth Multi-Workspace App
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a Bolt app with OAuth 2.0 configuration for multi-workspace deployment. Implement a database-backed installation store for persisting tokens, configure the required scopes minimally, and add the OAuth redirect routes for handling the installation flow.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing an app for public distribution

output_examples.input: User sends message 'hello bot' in a channel where the bot is present
output_examples.output: Bot responds with a personalized greeting: 'Hey there @username!' acknowledging the user by their Slack handle

output_examples.input: Incident alert triggered with critical database outage details
output_examples.output: Slack message displays red circle header, incident fields showing title and severity, description truncated to 2000 characters, and action buttons for Acknowledge, Escalate, and Resolve

output_examples.input: User types /ticket command and submits modal with title 'Login Issue', description 'Users cannot access portal', and priority 'High'
output_examples.output: Confirmation message posts to channel: 'Ticket created: Login Issue (High Priority)' with a unique ticket ID for tracking

best_practices: Request minimum required OAuth scopes to reduce user friction during installation and limit potential security exposure
Acknowledge all interactions within 3 seconds using ack() then process logic asynchronously to avoid timeout errors
Store tokens and secrets in environment variables or secure vaults, never hardcode or log credential values
anti_patterns: Hardcoding Slack tokens or signing secrets directly in source code instead of using environment variables
Requesting excessive OAuth scopes 'just in case' which increases security risk and reduces installation conversion
Processing events synchronously without acknowledgment which causes Slack to retry and duplicate events

faq.question: How do I create a new Slack app to use with this skill?
faq.answer: Visit https://api.slack.com/apps and click 'Create New App'. Choose 'From scratch' or 'From an app manifest'. After creation, navigate to 'OAuth & Permissions' to configure scopes and install the app to your workspace.

faq.question: Where do I find my Slack signing secret?
faq.answer: Go to your app settings at api.slack.com/apps, select 'Basic Information' from the left sidebar, and locate 'App Credentials'. Copy the 'Signing Secret' value and store it in your SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET environment variable.

faq.question: What is the difference between bot tokens and user tokens?
faq.answer: Bot tokens (xoxb-) represent your app and have scopes you configure. User tokens (xoxp-) represent individual users who install your app and inherit their permissions. Bot tokens are recommended for most automation scenarios.

faq.question: Do I need to host a public server to receive Slack events?
faq.answer: No. Use Socket Mode for development and testing, which receives events over WebSocket without public endpoints. For production, HTTP endpoints are more reliable but require a publicly accessible server with HTTPS.

faq.question: How do I test Block Kit layouts before implementing them?
faq.answer: Use the Block Kit Builder at app.slack.com/block-kit-builder to visually design and preview your message layouts. Export the JSON structure and integrate it into your Bolt app code.

faq.question: What happens if my app exceeds Slack API rate limits?
faq.answer: Slack returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header indicating seconds to wait. Implement exponential backoff retry logic in your app and consider batching operations or caching to reduce API calls.
